tata.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: FY2023 is consolidated while FY2024 is standalone

sections.about: This metric measures recovery not classified as recycling or re-use under BRSR Principle 6, Essential Indicator on recovery route. It typically captures sale as co-products, co-processing or other beneficial recovery routes. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: SAIL dominated this route with 11,249,128 MT in FY2025, reflecting its classification of major solid-waste utilisation streams as other recovery. Tata and JSW both reported zero in FY2024 and FY2025, while Jindal reported only 76.59 MT in FY2025 after 19,418.5 MT in FY2024. The difference appears largely classificatory: Tata’s circularity is captured under recycling/re-use, whereas SAIL’s slag and by-product utilisation is heavily captured under other recovery.
sections.tataPositioning: Tata ranked joint 3rd in FY2025 and was 100% behind SAIL because Tata reported zero other recovery. Tata’s rank fell from 2nd in FY2023 to joint 3rd in FY2024-FY2025 because Tata no longer reported material recovery under this route.
sections.targets: SAIL’s 100% solid waste utilisation aspiration is most relevant to this metric. Tata has no other-recovery-specific target identified in the retrieved BRSR extracts.
sections.comparabilityNotes: This metric is highly sensitive to classification choices between recycling, re-use and other recovery. Tata Steel FY2022-23 data is reported on a consolidated basis rather than standalone.

flags.flagNote: Tata FY2023-to-FY2024 trend is ambiguous; recovery-route classification differs materially across companies.
